observation_lifecycle_contract.purpose: Emit compact retrieval quality observations for candidate volume, usable evidence, coverage gaps, provider status, and synthesis handoff without storing raw provider payloads.

retry_query_strategy: split broad requests into entity, aspect, time-window, or source-type searches
target primary, official, source-backed, or directly citable pages when relevant
use low-relevance and off-topic diagnostics to remove weak terms
when provider degradation or partial failure is recorded, change source class, page type, exact entity terms, or reader/fetch depth instead of repeating the same broad query
when search rows are relevant but low-detail, prefer promising-page read/fetch enrichment before synthesis if policy and budget allow
synthesize from best recorded evidence only after internal recovery is exhausted

web_research_broker_contract.purpose: present web research as one general primitive made of admitted retrieval lanes, so agents can reason over search, read, crawl, extraction, ranking, coverage, and recovery without binding to provider-specific tools or output formats
web_research_broker_contract.synthesis_boundary: The broker report calibrates what happened and what remains weak; it does not prescribe visible answer structure, phrasing, or source conclusions.

browser_materialization_capability_contr.purpose: Provide an optional, measured page-materialization recovery lane when ordinary search/fetch providers are blocked, throttled, JavaScript-dependent, or too thin for synthesis.

progressive_retrieval_contract.objective: gather enough useful evidence before synthesis instead of forcing a final answer from structurally successful but weak search output

evidence_pack_contract.purpose: ranked synthesis context with source class, confidence, quality flags, coverage facets, claim hints, term hints, and freshness signals

ranking_policy.objective: pack evidence for coverage, authority, diversity, freshness, density, and novelty before synthesis
packing_rules: preserve coverage-critical evidence even when it is not globally top-ranked
prefer distinct source domains and source classes for broad research when available
keep weak raw evidence lower-confidence than usable evidence
include missing-facet diagnostics for verifier or retry planning

raw_result_retention_contract.purpose: retain weak raw provider evidence long enough for diagnostics, recovery planning, and bounded low-evidence synthesis
raw_result_retention_contract.synthesis_boundary: low_confidence_raw evidence is weaker than usable evidence and retrieval telemetry is not source evidence

bounded_second_pass_recovery.strategy: preserve the user goal, vary source class/page type/entity terms, enrich promising pages, and record remaining coverage gaps instead of treating provider telemetry as evidence

coverage_gap_recovery.purpose: recover from high-volume low-yield retrieval by targeting uncovered research facets instead of asking the user to narrow
coverage_gap_recovery.sufficiency_target: prefer at least three usable evidence refs and roughly three quarters of requested facets before treating high-volume retrieval as sufficient

candidate_locator_followup.purpose: When search rows expose promising public locators but snippets are thin, treat those locators as bounded page-read candidates so synthesis receives processible evidence instead of search-result shells.
candidate_locator_followup.selection: rank candidate locators with the same relevance/source/freshness axes used for payload links, dedupe canonical URLs, and fetch only within the declared per-stage and per-run budgets

semantic_query_planning.purpose: make query packs retrieve target evidence rather than repeat broad keywords

coverage_aware_query_planning.purpose: let the agent or policy gather more processible evidence by splitting broad research into coverage lanes before filtering

initial_discovery.purpose: cheap broad pass to learn vocabulary, candidate entities, source classes, and weak lanes

coverage_aware_evidence_promotion.purpose: promote evidence across the facets implied by the user request and submitted query pack before truncating to the final evidence window
coverage_aware_evidence_promotion.rule: facet-specific evidence should not be discarded solely because it ranks lower than globally broad evidence when it covers a requested research dimension

site_mapping.purpose: discover URL structure before selected content extraction or crawl

search_plus_read.purpose: promote search rows into selected page-read artifacts when snippets are not enough for synthesis

prefetch.purpose: discover links and candidate context without committing budget to full page extraction

best_first_candidate_expansion.purpose: spend follow-up fetches on the highest expected evidence gain

fit_content_packaging.purpose: provide compact evidence to synthesis while retaining raw payload out of chat

chunk_ranked_evidence.purpose: rank page sections before synthesis so large pages do not collapse into weak snippets

structured_artifact_packaging.purpose: preserve tables, links, media, metadata, and extracted structures as evidence signals without prescribing user-visible formatting

deterministic_structured_extraction.purpose: preserve repeatable page structures as evidence artifacts before semantic synthesis

search_then_fetch_escalation.purpose: treat search snippets and highlights as candidate evidence, then fetch promising URLs only when body context is needed

reader_representation_controls.purpose: declare evidence artifact representations and readiness metadata without constraining final chat structure

provider_result_envelope.purpose: retain safe provider metadata for ranking, coverage diagnostics, and synthesis calibration without leaking raw payloads

advanced_search_controls.purpose: declare optional retrieval controls that adapters may support without making them workflow routes

tool_admission_contract.purpose: keep simple search and fetch broadly available while advanced controls and provider-specific specialties remain admitted capabilities, not workflow branches

provider_error_contract.purpose: turn provider/transport failures into structured result quality metadata before recovery or synthesis

adaptive_state_projection.purpose: surface progress and stop reasons for multi-step retrieval without letting crawl state become chat-visible payload

final_response_verifier.purpose: declare the semantic quality checks that should run before final projection or trigger bounded retrieval/synthesis retry

async_result_lifecycle.purpose: support high-volume retrieval through handles, polling, and cleanup without making raw task payloads chat-visible

blocked_recovery_trace.purpose: tell synthesis whether retrieval failed because the web is low-signal or because access/readiness blocked the evidence path

retrieval_profile_selection.purpose: allow different extraction strategies without hardcoding domains or user prompt examples

dynamic_readiness_diagnostics.purpose: summarize why static fetch was incomplete without leaking raw network or console payloads

blocked_text_fragments: confirm this search was made by a human

query_metadata_contract.purpose: Let the agent submit compact retrieval metadata separately from prose queries so the primitive can cast a wider, auditable net without hardcoding a research domain or user-visible answer format.

accepted_fields.keywords: important entity, alias, phrase, aspect, source-class, date, or decision terms
accepted_fields.required_coverage.entities: named entities, candidates, subjects, or options final synthesis should cover or mark as weak/missing
accepted_fields.required_coverage.facets: user-stated criteria, aspects, source classes, time windows, or decision dimensions final synthesis should cover or mark as weak/missing
accepted_fields.aliases: alternate names or exact phrases for named entities
accepted_fields.negative_terms: ambiguity filters to exclude low-signal interpretations
accepted_fields.query_metadata_policy: request-path marker that explains whether metadata was expanded or intentionally narrow

compilation_policy: preserve the top-level user query as the first query lane
preserve agent-submitted query lanes before compiled metadata lanes
dedupe and cap metadata terms according to the active aperture budget
quote exact entity and alias phrases when compiling metadata lanes
do not quote generic facets or keywords by default
append safe negative filters only to compiled metadata lanes
record query_metadata, submitted_query_plan, executed query_plan, and query_plan_source in tool output

blocked_text_fragments: confirm this search was made by a human
confirm this search was made by a human
